codeReview.summary: The commit adds useful orchestrator helper coverage, but it does not actually complete the advertised PlanStatus/OrchestratorTaskStatus alignment and it also bundles unrelated release-version bumps into the refactor.

issues.description: In the reviewed commit, `plan.ts` still defines `PlanStatus = 'pending' | 'running' | 'done' | 'blocked'` and `PLAN_STATUSES` inline instead of consuming the new `CommonTaskStatus`. That means the feature did not actually align `PlanStatus` with `OrchestratorTaskStatus` or eliminate the duplicated status source of truth promised by the feature description.

This refactor commit also bumps the package version from 0.5.2 to 0.5.3 even though the feature is only about shared orchestrator types. Release metadata changes are unrelated side effects that should not be bundled into this refactor.
The Chrome extension manifest version is bumped from 0.5.2 to 0.5.3 in the same refactor commit. That changes shipped extension metadata without being part of this feature's scope.
The Firefox manifest version is also bumped from 0.5.2 to 0.5.3 as part of this shared-types refactor. This is another unrelated release-scoped change that should be removed from the feature commit.

The new test says `CommonTaskStatus` is compatible with both status types, but it only checks assignment to `OrchestratorTaskStatus` and never imports or exercises `PlanStatus`. That left the incomplete cross-module alignment unverified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker skill's final validation procedure omits `npm run check:repo-standards`, so a worker can follow the skill and still miss a repo-mandated guardrail before handoff.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Repo AGENTS.md says to run `npm run check:repo-standards` before handoff; `.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md` final validation only lists typecheck, related tests, lint, and build; this feature's handoff verification likewise reports `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run build`, and `npm run lint`, but no `check:repo-standards`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared architecture notes are stale for the shared package layout and no longer reflect the refactored shared-type modules workers now need to navigate.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md:94-103` still lists `runtime-message-definitions.ts`, `providers.ts`, `tools.ts`, and only a single `orchestrator.ts`, while the current `packages/shared/src/` tree contains modules such as `orchestrator-types.ts`, `task-status-helpers.ts`, `plan-builders.ts`, `provider-instance.ts`, and `connection-config.ts`.

summary: Review failed. The feature's commit added orchestrator-focused tests, but it did not actually wire `PlanStatus` into the new shared status base and it also included unrelated package/manifest version bumps.
